text:
There was an ancient elm tree in a tranquil English village. It had stood there for centuries, its branches stretching towards the sky like friendly fingers. One day, a young girl named Alice wandered into the village looking for adventure. She noticed the old elm and thought it might befriend her.

That afternoon, Alice leaned against its rough bark and imagined herself climbing up into its leaves. She imagined casting spells—a quiet adventure. Over time, she would share her stories with the elm: tales of heroism, fantastical creatures, and adventures abroad. The tree listened intently, its leaves rustling softly.

Years turned into decades. Alice grew older but her visits to the elm grew more frequent. She would spend hours there, telling tales into the twilight, the only creature awake at those hours. The tree grew stronger. Its leaves shimmered in the moonlight, and cracks formed deep along its trunk like ancient scars.
